Title: Petra Energy unit wins two-year Sarawak PSC contract
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petra Energy Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary has secured a contract to provide field operations management services for an offshore production sharing contract (PSC) in Sarawak.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the group said Petra Energy Development Sdn Bhd had received a letter of award from Vestigo Petroleum Sdn Bhd for the SK407 PSC and its associated facilities.
The SK407 field is located offshore Miri, Sarawak, and comprises five offshore fields, namely West Lutong, Baram, Tukau, Siwa and Fairly Baram.
"There is no fixed value stated in the letter of award. The duration of the contract is two years from April 1, 2026, to March 31, 2028," it said.
Petra Energy said the award is expected to contribute positively to the group's earnings and net assets per share over the contract period.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rescued kidnapping victim turns out to be Interpol Red Notice subject
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have rescued a kidnapped South Korean man, only to later discover that he is wanted in his home country.
Federal police Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the 40-year-old was abducted while travelling from Genting Highlands on April 17 before being rescued at a homestay in Melaka.
"In the incident, the victim's car was followed and intercepted by another vehicle, before he was pulled out, assaulted, tied up and forced into the suspects' vehicle. He was then taken to an unknown location," he said at a press conference at Bukit Aman today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Australian woman found safe after Mount Santubong hiking ordeal
Article:
KUCHING: An Australian woman has been rescued after spending a night in a deep ravine on Mount Santubong in Sarawak after straying from a hiking trail.
Kuching police chief Assistant Commissioner Alexson Naga Chabu said the victim, Belinda Lapier, in her 40s, was located by rescue teams in a ravine about 700m deep at 11.59am.
He said efforts were under way to bring her out.
"The terrain is very steep, and it is expected to take two to three hours to evacuate her as rescuers have to navigate difficult trails," he said.
"The victim is not injured, but she is unable to walk due to exhaustion after spending the night in the ravine."
Mount Santubong is a coastal mountain near Kuching, rising to about 810m and popular with hikers for its rainforest trails and views of the South China Sea.
Despite its accessibility, parts of the route are steep and rugged, with marked sections such as the F7 trail requiring care, particularly in low light or wet conditions.
Alexson said 24 personnel, including firefighters, police, the Malaysian armed forces, Sarawak Forestry Department rangers and members of the public, were involved in the search and rescue operation.
He said the 810m mountain was climbed by the victim and 14 other hikers yesterday morning.
However, she became separated from the group during the descent in the afternoon.
Earlier, a Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesman said the victim was located at about 10.30am after she responded when rescuers called out her name.
Rescuers faced
difficulties
reaching her due to obstacles along the route and the near-vertical slope of the ravine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Murder probe opened over fatal drink-driving crash involving soldier on MEX
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have opened a murder investigation into a military personnel arrested over suspected drink-driving following a fatal crash on the Maju Expressway in Kuala Lumpur early yesterday.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the suspect was brought to the Dang Wangi district police headquarters today, where an application for remand was made to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
More to come ...
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM230mil NGO fund probe: MACC tracing money flow from banks, says Azam
Article:
NILAI: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is tracing the flow of funds in and out of accounts to complete investigations into the alleged misappropriation of about RM230 million involving a non-governmental organisation (NGO).
MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the commission was obtaining the necessary information from financial institutions and expects to receive the records within a week.
He said the bank statements would be crucial in helping investigators complete the probe.
"This case does not only involve transactions in 2025 or 2026. We are looking at earlier records, including those from 2024 and even as far back as 2018.
"So it may take some time, but we are confident the banks will provide the required documents," he said after witnessing the signing of a memorandum of agreement (MoA) between the MACC and the Enforcement, Leadership and Management University (Elmu) at Bandar Enstek here.
The MACC was represented by acting commissioner Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar, while Elmu was represented by chief financial officer Datin Noraini Aripin. Also present was Elmu vice-chancellor Datuk Dr Kassim Mohamed.
Yesterday, it was reported that the MACC had seized assets worth
more than RM18.3 million
to assist in the investigation into the alleged misappropriation of NGO funds.
The seizures involved 14 properties, comprising seven residential units, three office spaces and four plots of land at several locations.
The MACC also confirmed that
total asset seizures under Op Sutra
involving three people — including a deputy chairman of an NGO — had so far reached RM8.73 million.
Azam said investigations to date indicated that the funds were misused for personal purposes, including the purchase of assets.
"We are gathering further information and verifying bank statements to determine how much money was channelled into personal accounts.
"Anyone found to have received the funds, including family members, will be called in to assist investigations. Once we obtain the bank records, more individuals may be summoned," he said, adding that Pertubuhan Ikram Malaysia had also been called to assist.
Azam also said there was a need for a new mechanism or legislation to regulate and monitor funds received by NGOs, adding that similar cases had occurred previously.
"At present, there are no specific laws to monitor funds received by NGOs through various platforms.
"This has led to instances of abuse, including exploitation of religious sentiments, where public donations are misused.
"In my view, this is a serious issue that must be addressed moving forward. The MACC will work with relevant parties to identify the best approach.
"There is a need for specific legislation to regulate such funds, as cases of abuse of power, fraud and criminal breach of trust have been identified," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six shots fired in Johor killings, suspect to be charged Monday
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU: At least six shots are believed to have been fired in a shooting at a restaurant in Taman Kota Jaya on April 19, which left three people dead.
Johor police chief Ab Rahaman Arsad said investigators recovered six bullet casings at the scene.
He said the 71-year-old suspect is believed to have acted over a debt dispute involving RM50,000 with one of the victims, a 37-year-old Vietnamese woman.
"The suspect is believed to have lent the money in stages since 2023 to help the woman manage her eatery. However, there was no written agreement between them.
"The woman is believed to be running the eatery owned by her in-laws, while the other two deceased were regular customers, aged 61 and 63, and were at the scene when the incident occurred," he told a press conference at the Johor police headquarters today.
Investigations also found that the suspect holds a valid firearm licence, issued in Kuantan, Pahang.
Ab Rahaman said the suspect has been remanded until April 26 to assist in investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
"The investigation paper has been submitted to the Johor Public Prosecutor's Office, and we have been instructed to charge the suspect at the Kota Tinggi Court with three counts of murder," he said, adding that this is expected to take place on April 27.
He added that the suspect tested negative for drugs and was not under the influence of alcohol at the time of the incident.
In the 1.30pm incident, two men and a foreign woman were found dead after being shot at the restaurant, believed to have been carried out using a shotgun.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: May 14 decision on Zahid's bid for acquittal in Yayasan Akalbudi case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The decision on Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's application to be discharged and acquitted of 47 charges of criminal breach of trust, corruption and money laundering involving Yayasan Akalbudi funds will be delivered on May 14.
High Court Judge Nurulhuda Nur'aini Mohamad Nor fixed the date after counsel Datuk Hamidi Mohd Noh, representing Zahid, informed the court that the defence had applied to vacate today's decision date pending the Court of Appeal's ruling on May 7.
"The court sets May 14 for the decision, subject to the outcome of the Court of Appeal on May 7," she said.
Earlier, Hamidi said that on April 20, the defence had filed a letter seeking to vacate today's decision date pending the appellate court's ruling.
He added that Zahid was exempted from attending today's proceedings.
The proceedings were also attended by Deputy Public Prosecutor Mohamad Shahrizzat Armadan and lawyer Collin Andrew, who held a watching brief for the Malaysian Bar.
The Bar filed an appeal against the High Court's decision to reject the application for leave to appeal to challenge the Attorney General's (AG) decision discontinuing proceedings in Zahid's case.
On Feb 24, the prosecution did not object to Zahid's application to be discharged and acquitted of the charges, but Justice Nurulhuda said that the court would not make a decision on the application until the Court of Appeal's decision in the case involving the Malaysian Bar.
The Bar, as amicus curiae (friend of the court), submitted a letter of objection to Zahid's application on the same date (Feb 24), but this was objected to by lawyer Datuk Hisyam Teh Poh Teik.
Zahid filed the full acquittal application on the grounds that the Attorney-General's Chambers (AGC) had, through a media statement, decided that there was no further action (NFA) on all 47 charges.
On Jan 8, the AGC, in a media statement, said that there would be no further action on the 47 charges after reviewing new materials and evidence.
Subsequently, on Jan 12, Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar said the decision to classify the case as NFA was made only after the prosecution had carefully examined six representations submitted by the Umno president.
He stressed that the decision was not made arbitrarily, adding that the AGC would not classify any case as NFA without a thorough assessment of the facts, evidence and legal merits.
On Sept 4, 2023, the High Court here discharged not amounting to an acquittal Zahid on the 47 charges after the prosecution informed the court that it would discontinue proceedings.
The decision was delivered by then High Court judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, who has since been elevated to the Federal Court.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hospital staff remanded over RM54,000 false claims probe
Article:
KUANTAN: A hospital staff member has been remanded in connection with a probe into false claims involving surgical equipment worth about RM54,000.
The man in his 40s, a civil servant serving at a hospital in Pahang, was remanded until tomorrow after an application by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission at the Raub Magistrate's Court today.
Magistrate Siti Aisyah Ahmad issued the remand order.
A source said the suspect was detained yesterday after turning up at the Raub MACC office at about 10pm to have his statement recorded.
"Investigations revealed that the suspect had abetted another man, a sales manager at a healthcare supplies company.
The first suspect was detained on Tuesday.
"They are being investigated for submitting documents containing false particulars to the Orang Asli Development Department in Lipis for the supply of surgical equipment," the source said.
Pahang MACC director Mohd Shukor Mahmud confirmed the arrest, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.
The offence is punishable under Section 24(2) of the same Act, which carries a maximum jail term of 20 years and a fine of five times the value of the offence or RM10,000, whichever is higher.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Microsoft targets 800,000 Malaysians for AI upskilling
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Microsoft aims to equip 800,000 Malaysians with AI skills through the AIForMYFuture initiative, building upon the Microsoft Elevate programme.
The global technology giant, through Microsoft Elevate has already empowered 80,000 participants to date, while reaching 1.53 million people under the Bersama Malaysia programme, which covers cloud skills, AI and cybersecurity.
Supported by the National AI Office and the National TVET Council, the initiative supports Malaysia's ambition to become a regional AI hub by 2030 by focusing on awareness, access, and adoption across all sectors of society.
The launch of Microsoft Elevate marks the expansion of a national AI capacity building initiative aimed at strengthening Malaysia's readiness for AI, in line with the country's ambition to become an AI nation by 2030.
Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said Microsoft's commitment, together with the government, complements Malaysia's efforts to become a regional AI hub, with digital capability development serving as a key driver that benefits all levels of society.
"To ensure meaningful progress at the national level, close collaboration between the public and private sectors must be strengthened.
"We therefore welcome Microsoft Elevate, an initiative supported by the National AI Office (NAIO) and the National TVET Council Secretariat, and implemented by Biji-Biji Initiative and Mereka to enhance AI readiness and digital inclusion so that Malaysians are prepared for an AI driven future," he told reporters after launching Microsoft Elevate here today.
Earlier in his speech, Gobind said Malaysia's ambition to become an AI nation by 2030 depends on ensuring every citizen is ready to understand, access, and adopt AI.
He said Microsoft's collaboration with the government supports the broader national agenda of building an inclusive AI ecosystem, where technology improves productivity, lowers costs, and creates economic opportunities across society.
Gobind said the government's focus is centred on three priorities, namely awareness, access, and adoption, to help Malaysians and businesses use AI meaningfully while ensuring the necessary infrastructure and industry ecosystem are in place.
He added that affordable access to AI tools is critical so that communities, workers, and enterprises are not left behind as digital transformation accelerates.
He said stronger cooperation between the public and private sectors will be key to building a trusted, ethical, and competitive AI ecosystem, while positioning Malaysia as a regional AI hub.
Microsoft Elevate is being carried out in collaboration with the National AI Office, the National TVET Council Secretariat, and Biji-Biji Initiative and Mereka, reflecting a whole-of-nation commitment to ensure every segment of Malaysian society is prepared for the AI era.
The initiative expands the national skills agenda to include educators, micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), military retirees, educational institutions, and civil servants.
This approach reflects a nationwide effort to ensure every segment of society is ready for the era of AI powered technology.
Microsoft Elevate is designed to expand upskilling opportunities in Malaysia through a phased roadmap leading up to 2030.
Implementation begins with skills activation and workforce development before progressing to sector deepening, institutionalisation, and long-term sustainability.
Microsoft Asean general manager for legal and government affairs Dr Jasmine Begum said AI can only generate real value when it is understood and used meaningfully across all levels of society.
She said technology serves as an enabler, while its true impact depends on how individuals, organisations, and institutions apply AI in practical, trusted, and relevant ways to address real world needs.
She added that Microsoft Elevate is designed to build capabilities at scale and support communities, businesses, and public sector institutions in adopting AI confidently and responsibly.
"Through this programme, we aim to ensure Malaysia's AI ambitions are realised by creating long-term impact, enabling more people to gain the skills, access, and support needed to thrive in the AI economy," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bring them home: Protesters demand return of elephants from Japan
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A group of animal lovers today called for the return of three Malaysian elephants — Dara, Amoi and Kelat — from Japan, citing concerns over their wellbeing following their relocation last month.
About 20 demonstrators, led by the Sekretariat Bahana Kesetiaan Negara, held a peaceful gathering at the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry headquarters, where they submitted a memorandum urging the authorities to bring the elephants home.
The protest follows an online petition that has garnered more than 100,000 signatures calling for the animals' return.
Social activist Kalai Vanar, who represented the group, said the memorandum was received by ministry officials, including the ministry's deputy secretary-general Datuk Mas Rizal Mohd Hilmi and Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) deputy director-general (conservation) Datuk Fakhrul Hatta Musa.
"We have no political interests. We are here solely for the animals. The climate there is not suitable for them.
"We were told the matter will be conveyed to the minister, who is aware of the issue and is expected to respond soon.
"We want an official statement within three days. What we want is to bring Dara, Amoi and Kelat home. Kelat is currently injured," he told reporters.
The elephants were
relocated in March
under a collaboration between Zoo Taiping & Night Safari and Tennoji Zoo in Osaka.
During a recent Perak Legislative Assembly sitting, state housing and local government committee chairman Sandrea Ng clarified that the transfer was not a sale, with ownership retained by Zoo Taiping.
She said the relocation, planned since 2021, had received approval from Perhilitan and complied with the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (Cites) as well as national conservation laws.
However, demonstrators questioned the need to relocate the animals overseas.
Norlia Samsi, 53, said Malaysia had suitable facilities and natural conditions to care for the elephants.
"Is there a clear timeline for their return? We have not been told whether it will be 25 years or otherwise.
"If caretakers from Malaysia are needed on rotation, why not keep them here? Elephants are intelligent and emotional animals. We can already see signs of physical injury, but what about stress?" said the Subang Jaya-based businesswoman.
Another demonstrator, Linda Mamat, 50, claimed Tennoji Zoo was not a suitable environment for the elephants.
"The climate is different. We already have proper conservation facilities such as Kuala Gandah in Pahang. They should have remained there," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: List of MACC chief candidates submitted to King
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A list of candidates for the post of Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner has been presented to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, for consideration, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mother seeks RM5k for son's urgent surgery after crash
Article:
TUMPAT: A 24-year-old man remains bedridden in Johor after a road accident 10 days ago left him seriously injured.
Mohamad Amirullah Mohd Shaferi sustained severe injuries, including head trauma, a fractured jaw and a broken left shoulder.
His mother, Zabariah Hassan, said Amirullah had recently undergone surgery to remove blood clots and treat a head infection, and would require further procedures, resulting in significant costs.
Zabariah said her son urgently needs jaw surgery, but the family is struggling to afford it.
"Doctors have informed us that he needs surgery to repair his jaw as soon as possible, but it will cost RM5,000, which we are unable to afford immediately.
"They also said the procedure cannot proceed until payment is made,," she told Harian Metro when met at her rented house in Kampung Telaga Lanas, Pengkalan Kubor.
Zabariah said the family is struggling to raise funds for the surgeries, as her husband works as a cleaner with the Tumpat Municipal Council.
She said Amirullah, who works at a factory in Pasir Gudang, Johor, was riding pillion on a motorcycle with a friend when it skidded on the way home from work, killing his friend and leaving him seriously injured
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Post-mortem completed for Bangladeshi tourist killed in MEX crash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The post-mortem examination on Bangladeshi tourist Muzahid Millad, who was killed in a suspected drink-driving crash early yesterday, has been completed.
A hospital spokesperson said his remains were pending collection by his family.
"The post-mortem was completed yesterday," the spokesperson said at Kuala Lumpur Hospital today.
Muzahid, 22, was killed when the
e-hailing vehicle carrying him and his wife
from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) was struck along the Maju Expressway (MEX).
His wife, Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, 23, was injured and is receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital.
The couple from Habiganj had arrived in Malaysia for a holiday and were planning to spend a week in Kuala Lumpur.
In a post on Muzahid's YouTube channel, Advance Gaming, which has more than 777,000 subscribers, it was stated: "With deep sorrow, it is being shared that
Advance Gaming, or Muzahid, passed away
this morning (April 23) following an accident in Malaysia."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Terengganu schools halt outdoor activities amid hot, dry spell
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: All schools in Terengganu have been instructed to suspend outdoor activities due to the ongoing hot and dry weather.
Terengganu State Education Department director Kamsah @ Kamal Mohamed said a directive had been issued to all schools and took effect from April 20.
"All outdoor activities have been cancelled, including water-based activities, while indoor activities may proceed. Other programmes such as the 1 Student 1 Sport initiative, uniformed units, clubs and associations, as well as sports and games, are also suspended.
"This decision was made to ease the current situation and ensure safety. The safety of students is a priority that cannot be compromised.
"Assemblies are only allowed in the early morning. Any programmes scheduled in the afternoon must be postponed," he said when contacted today.
He added that fires reported in several areas, particularly in Kuala Nerus, and the resulting haze are being closely monitored by the department.
"I have contacted the state Health Department deputy director and the District Health Office to seek advice on the current situation.
"Posters on preventive measures have also been distributed to all schools. For now, there is no need to implement Home-Based Teaching and Learning (PdPR) as the Department of Environment and the District Health Office have indicated that the situation is under control," he said.
Kamsah said further instructions would be issued from time to time in line with guidelines set by the Education Ministry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Careless lane change leads to motorcycle rear-end collision
Article:
PETALING JAYA: A viral dashcam video showing a motorcycle being rear-ended by a car along Jalan 16/11 here has prompted police to open an investigation into the incident.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsuddin Mamat said the incident occurred at 7.10am yesterday, while a report was lodged at 3pm.
He said the motorcycle, ridden by the victim with a pillion passenger, was travelling in the left lane before suddenly moving into the right lane.
"Due to the close distance, a car travelling in the right lane was unable to avoid the collision and struck the rear of the motorcycle," he said in a statement today.
Both rider and pillion were thrown onto the road following the impact.
The driver, a local woman, stopped to assist the victims and later took them to a clinic for treatment before lodging a police report.
He said investigations were ongoing under Rule 6(1) of the Road Traffic Rules (LN 166/59).
Police also reminded road users to comply with traffic regulations, adding that firm action would be taken against those who drive dangerously.
Earlier, a 27-second dashcam clip of the incident went viral on social media, showing the motorcycle being hit from behind by a car.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trial over Genting founder's late daughter inheritance dispute adjourned to June
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today postponed the trial over the Genting inheritance dispute involving the late Lim Siew Kim's will and her two daughters to June.
Lawyer Datuk Seri V Sithambaram, representing the siblings, requested a deferral, citing time constraints as the court was engaged in another ongoing trial.
The proceedings were delayed as judge Mahazan Mat Taib was presiding over another case.
"We appreciate that the court is also tied up with a full trial where submissions are ongoing.
"I speak for all defendants' lawyers and have their consent to seek an adjournment to June 8," he said.
June 8 was an earlier fixed date for the trial.
Sithambaram added that he would be moving into another area of cross-examination involving the main will, which would require considerable time.
Lawyers for all four defendants indicated that they had no objections to the adjournment.
Mahazan allowed the application, saying it would be appropriate to continue the proceedings on a fresh date.
"As the plaintiff's lawyer indicated he will be addressing another point on the main will, it is better to have a fresh day for that," she said.
Sithambaram represents Chan T'shiao Li, 48, and Kimberly Chan Tshiao Miin, 45, who are contesting their late mother's will involving assets worth more than RM1.6bn.
T'shiao Li was left RM900,000, while Kimberly received RM100,000.
In contrast, their younger brother, Marcus Chan Jau Chwen, 42, was bequeathed properties in Jalan Ampang, shareholding in Mantap Awana Sdn Bhd, contents of all safe deposit boxes, and 30 per cent of Siew Kim's residuary estate.
Their elder sister, Cressa Chan T'shiao Yunn, was left RM10 million and property in Ampang Hilir, Kuala Lumpur, while her daughter, Jasmine, is to receive RM50,000 a month.
T'shiao Li and Kimberly are challenging the validity of the will, arguing that their mother lacked testamentary capacity due to her medical condition before her death.
Siew Kim, who died of cancer in July 2022 at the age of 73, had her third and final will drafted, prepared and executed by lawyer Datuk Low Beng Choo on April 28, 2022.
She was the third child of Tan Sri Lim Goh Tong, founder of the Genting group of companies.
The siblings claimed that Siew Kim did not have the legal and mental capacity to make a valid will due to stage four ovarian cancer.
They also claimed to have discovered in 2023 the existence of two other wills made by their mother prior to the one drafted in April 2022.
The other two wills were dated November 2, 2021, and April 11, 2022. The third will was executed on April 28, 2022.
They further claimed that the execution of the third will within a short period amounted to suspicious circumstances surrounding the preparation and making of the final will.
The first and second defendants are Malcolm Fernandez and Chan Mei Yee, the executors and trustees named in Siew Kim's will dated April 28, 2022.
The third and fourth defendants are Marcus and Low respectively.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran fast-boat swarms add to Hormuz threats for shipping
Article:
LONDON: Iran's use of a swarm of small, fast boats to seize two container ships near the Strait of Hormuz could undermine suggestions US forces have disabled its naval threat and reveals the challenges facing reopening one of the world's most important oil export routes.
US President Donald Trump on Monday acknowledged that while Iran's conventional navy had been largely destroyed, its "fast-attack ships" had not been considered much of a threat.
He said any such vessels coming near a US blockade set up outside the strait would be "immediately ELIMINATED" using the "same system of kill" deployed in the Caribbean and Pacific where US air strikes have hit suspected drug boats and killed at least 110 people.
Those boats were not attacking large, unarmed commercial ships, however, nor nearly as heavily armed, with Iran's Revolutionary Guards packing heavy machineguns, rocket launchers and, in some cases, anti-ship missiles.
Speedboat attacks now form part of a "layered system of threats," alongside "shore-based missiles, drones, mines and electronic interference to create uncertainty and slow decision-making," Greek maritime security company Diaplous told Reuters.
Iran was estimated to have hundreds, if not thousands, of these boats before the war, often hidden in coastal tunnels, naval bases or among civilian vessels, according to maritime security specialists.
Some 100 or more may have been destroyed since the Iran war began of Feb 28, said Corey Ranslem, chief executive of maritime security group Dryad Global.
CHANGE IN TACTICS
Before this week, Iran had relied on missile and drone strikes to hit shipping traffic around the strait, a route which normally handles 20 per cent of the world's daily oil and liquefied natural gas supply.
Those attacks had stopped with the April 8 ceasefire.
The seizure of the two container ships by Iran followed Washington imposing a blockade on Iran's trade by sea and the start of it intercepting Iran-linked oil tankers and other ships.
"The civilian shipping industry is not equipped to prevent Iranian armed forces from seizing vessels," said Daniel Mueller, a senior analyst at British maritime security company Ambrey.
Typically, about a dozen boats are used in a seizure operation, he added.
Iran's fast boats now serve as the "backbone" of Iran's naval strategy, able to deploy rapidly as part of its "asymmetrical war against the enemy," a senior Iranian security official told Reuters.
"Because of their very high speeds, these boats can successfully carry out hit-and-run attacks without being detected," the official said, speaking on condition of anonymity.
FAST BOAT LIMITATIONS
Including this week's seizures, Iran has used small, fast boats at least seven times going back to 2019, Ambrey's Mueller said.
High winds and swells in the waters off Iran during summer make it hard to conduct such operations, said one Iranian shipping source familiar with the waters.
"When it is very bumpy, they (armed forces onboard) cannot shoot," the source said.
They are also ill-equipped to go head to head with a warship, and would likely suffer "very heavy casualties" in any direct assault on one, said Jeremy Binnie, a Middle East specialist at defence intelligence company Janes.
"Even if they tried to saturate the ship's defences by attacking from multiple directions, they would be extremely vulnerable to the air support that would be called in," he said.
On paper, guided missile strikes would easily destroy these boats, but shoulder-fired missile launchers would pose a threat to low-flying US aircraft, Binnie said.
"It is going to be much harder to eliminate the small boat threat than it was to destroy Iran's larger naval vessels, which were big targets that were relatively easy to find and track and, at most, only had a limited ability to defend themselves from air attack," he said.
The reality for the shipping sector is further disruption as well as elevated insurance costs.
After the so-called "tanker war" of the 1980s, Iran increasingly used asymmetric tactics as the Iranian navy was effectively destroyed, much as it has been in the current conflict, said Duncan Potts, a director with consultancy Universal Defence and Security Solutions and a former British Royal Navy vice admiral.
"When the US Navy and the president say, 'We've destroyed the navy, we've sunk a frigate off Sri Lanka' – you've done that before, but you've forgotten that your opposition here went asymmetric. And they've perfected it." - Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China's DeepSeek says releases long-awaited new AI model
Article:
BEIJING: Chinese startup DeepSeek released a new artificial intelligence (AI) model Friday, more than a year after it stunned the world with a low-cost reasoning model that matched the capabilities of US rivals.
DeepSeek-V4 "features an ultra-long context of one million words", the company said in a statement on social media platform WeChat, hailing it as "cost-effective" in a separate announcement on X.
The announcement came as Meta said it planned to cut a tenth of its staff as it looks for productivity gains from the rest of the workforce while investing heavily in AI. Reports said Microsoft was also looking to trim its ranks.
DeepSeek-V4's context length, which determines how much input a model is able to absorb to help it complete tasks, "(achieves) leadership in both domestic and open-source fields across agent capabilities, world knowledge, and reasoning performance".
A "preview version" of the open source model is now available, the company said.
DeepSeek-V4 is released as two versions, DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash, with the latter being "a more efficient and economical choice" because it has smaller parameters.
V4-Pro has 1.6 trillion parameters while the V4-Flash has 284 billion parameters, which refine models' decision-making ability.
The model has also been "optimised" for popular AI Agent products such as Claude Code, OpenClaw, OpenCode and CodeBuddy, the statement said.
"In world knowledge benchmarks, DeepSeek-V4-Pro significantly leads other open-source models and is only slightly outperformed by the top-tier closed-source model, (Google's) Gemini-Pro-3.1," the statement added.
Hangzhou-based DeepSeek burst onto the scene in January last year with a generative AI chatbot, powered by its R1 reasoning model, that upended assumptions of US dominance in the strategic sector.
This so-called "DeepSeek shock" sparked a sell-off of AI-related shares and a reckoning on business strategy in what was also described as a "Sputnik moment" for the industry.
The chatbot performed at a similar level to ChatGPT and other top American offerings, but the company said it had taken significantly less computing power to develop.
However, its sudden popularity raised questions over data privacy and censorship, with the chatbot often refusing to answer questions on sensitive topics such as the 1989 Tiananmen crackdown.
At home, DeepSeek's AI tools have been widely adopted by Chinese municipalities and healthcare institutions as well as the financial sector and other businesses.
This has been partly driven by DeepSeek's decision to make its systems open source, with their inner workings public -- in contrast to the proprietary models sold by OpenAI and other Western rivals.
"China-made large AI models spearheaded the development of the global open-source AI ecosystem," Chinese Premier Li Qiang told an annual gathering of China's top decision-makers last month.
The AI race has intensified the rivalry between China and the United States, and the White House on Thursday accused Chinese entities of a massive effort to steal AI technology.
"The US has evidence that foreign entities, primarily in China, are running industrial-scale distillation campaigns to steal American AI," science and technology chief Michael Kratsios said in a post on X.
"We will be taking action to protect American innovation."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold set for weekly drop as higher oil prices stoke inflation fears
Article:
NEW YORK: Gold prices fell on Friday and were on track for a weekly drop, as elevated oil prices fuelled fears of inflation and higher-for-longer interest rates amid stalled US-Iran peace talks.
Spot gold was down 0.7 per cent at US$4,661.33 per ounce, as of 0426 GMT. The metal is down 3.5 per cent so far this week after a four-week winning run.
US gold futures for June delivery fell one per cent to US$4,676.50.
Brent crude prices have risen over 17 per cent so far this week to hover above US$105 a barrel, as the key Strait of Hormuz remained largely closed despite an extension of the Iran ceasefire.
As long as this risk of prolonged closure of the strait is there, oil will continue to trade at elevated levels, pressuring gold prices, said Kelvin Wong, a senior market analyst at OANDA.
Higher crude oil prices can stoke inflation by raising transportation and production costs, increasing the likelihood of higher interest rates.
While gold is considered an inflation hedge, high interest rates make yield-bearing assets more attractive, weighing on bullion's appeal.
"Gold is still being trapped in this sideways range, between the 50-day moving average at around US$4,900 and at the bottom, the 20-day moving average at US$4,645 level," Wong said, adding that "everything now boils down to what's going on in the Middle East."
Iran flaunted its tightened grip over the strait on Thursday with a video of commandos in a speedboat storming a huge cargo ship, after the collapse of peace talks that Washington had hoped would open one of the world's most important shipping corridors.
Trump told reporters that he believed Tehran wanted to make a deal but that its leadership was in turmoil. He said he was in no hurry for a deal, but if Iran did not want one, "I'll finish it up militarily."
The US dollar is up 0.8 per cent so far this week, making greenback-priced bullion more expensive for other currency holders.
The benchmark 10-year US Treasury yields have gained over two per cent this week, increasing the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding bullion.
Spot silver fell one per cent to US$74.69 per ounce, platinum lost 1.1 per cent to US$1,984.60, while palladium was down 0.3 per cent at US$1,464.02.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King Charles to visit US as political ties fray under Trump
Article:
LONDON: King Charles III heads to the United States on Monday with transatlantic tensions over the Iran war and the Epstein scandal's long shadow threatening to intrude on the landmark visit.
Both Buckingham Palace and the British government have said the four-day trip will honour the historic relationship between the two countries as the US marks 250 years of independence.
Charles's first US state visit as monarch comes at the request of the UK government and President Donald Trump and will be made with Queen Camilla, according to the palace.
However, as the American leader's war with Iran drives a rare wedge between London and Washington, it has generated considerable controversy.
Trump has repeatedly lambasted British Prime Minister Keir Starmer over his war opposition, alongside his government's immigration and energy policies.
"This is not Winston Churchill that we're dealing with," Trump grumbled in March, adding the so-called special relationship was "not like it used to be." The American leader has also mocked the perceived state of Britain's armed forces – of which the king is commander-in-chief.
In turn, Starmer has stepped up his public criticism of the war, while stressing the breadth and depth of UK-US ties in defending the state visit. An early April YouGov poll found 48 percent of Britons support cancelling it.
"Often what the monarchy is able to do, through the bonds that they build, is reach through the decades in a situation like this," Starmer told MPs when asked why the trip was going ahead.
Trump – a vocal admirer of the royals whose mother was Scottish – told the BBC on Thursday the visit could "absolutely" help repair relations, praising the king as "fantastic."
The UK's freshly-appointed ambassador to the US Christian Turner, whose predecessor Peter Mandelson was dismissed last year after revelations about his ties to Epstein, said the state visit was "important" for the countries' ties.
The king's relationship with the president "is a very personal one, the deep affection and respect they hold", Turner told NBC News.
'POLITICS'
Charles, 77, showcased his diplomatic skills during Trump's state visit to Britain last September, with Royal Holloway University of London monarchy expert Craig Prescott noting he is "generally very good" at navigating such occasions.
Prescott said the independence anniversary provided a useful "get out" for the British side to argue the trip is "not about Keir Starmer and Donald Trump, per se."
But he acknowledged it was "that little bit closer to politics" than usual and Charles would likely address the "very big elephant in the room" in a coded way in his speech Tuesday to the US Congress.
The first British monarch to address Congress since his mother, the late queen Elizabeth II, in 1991, he will mark the two countries' shared history and deep ties, the palace has said.
"He might refer to how the special relationship has perhaps waxed and waned over time," Prescott predicted, arguing Charles has proven to be a "better speaker" than his mother.
But Graham Smith, of the anti-monarchy campaign group Republic, argued the king will "go through the rituals without offering anything of substance."
The only critical thing "about this pointless trip is how Trump behaves," he said on X.
Meanwhile, the scandal around late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein threatens to encroach on the highly choreographed tour.
Charles has faced a major crisis over the friendship his brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, previously known as prince Andrew, had with the late billionaire, who died in prison in 2019.
The longstanding controversy intensified after Andrew was arrested in mid-February following new revelations over their links.
'OUT OF TOUCH'
The king, who stripped his disgraced brother of his titles in October, said "the law must take its course" in a rare personally signed statement.
Andrew, who remains under police investigation, has not been charged and has denied any wrongdoing.
Several US lawmakers have unsuccessfully urged the ex-prince to testify before Congress about Epstein.
Democrat Ro Khanna wrote to Charles in March requesting the king meet victims privately, while the family of late Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre made a similar demand.
Palace sources have told UK media a meeting "will not be possible", arguing it could "impact on (police) inquiries, or the proper course of the law."
Khanna told The Times this month that declining would make Charles look "out of touch" and "diminishes the credibility of the monarchy for future generations."
But he added acknowledging the survivors and their struggle for justice in his Congress address "would go a long way."
The visit, which will see the royals have tea with Trump and first lady Melania and attend a state dinner, appears meticulously planned to avoid unscripted moments.
Only photographers will capture Tuesday's Oval Office meeting between Trump and Charles, limiting chances for the king to be blindsided by the unpredictable US leader and reporters.
Charles and Camilla will visit New York on Wednesday, touring the 9/11 memorial, before departing Thursday for Bermuda for his first visit to a British overseas territory as monarch. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over 5,000 litres of diesel seized in Sarikei raid
Article:
SARIKEI: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry seized 5,030 litres of diesel during an enforcement operation at a premises in Sungai Mador, Bintangor, yesterday.
Sarawak director Matthew Dominic Barin said an investigation paper has been opened under Section 21 of the Control of Supplies Act 1961 for possessing or controlling scheduled goods in circumstances giving rise to suspicion of an offence.
"Inspections found that 5,030 litres of diesel were stored at the location without valid authorisation from the Controller of Supplies," he said in a statement.
He said a lorry, six intermediate bulk containers (IBC), 17 jerry cans, three electric pumps and eight rubber hoses were also seized.
"The seized diesel is estimated to be worth RM10,814.50, while the total value of the seizure, including equipment and the vehicle, is RM16,044.50," he said.
He urged the public to channel information on such activities through the ministry's official complaint platforms, including WhatsApp, email, the call centre line and the Ez ADU KPDN application.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Home Ministry to engage publishers before acting on contentious titles
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Home Ministry will strengthen its engagement with the publishing industry, including holding preliminary discussions with publishers before taking action against their publications.
The ministry said the move followed an engagement session with the Malaysian Book Publishers Association on April 21, where both sides discussed current issues and developments in the industry.
It described the approach as part of ongoing efforts to improve processes and ensure they are more transparent and constructive.
"At the same time, the ministry will review and reassess prohibition orders on publications that have been gazetted, taking into account views and suggestions from stakeholders.
"The Home Ministry respects and welcomes freedom of creative expression and intellectual discourse, and appreciates the development of writing creativity in the country," it said in a statement today.
However, the ministry stressed that all publications must comply with legal provisions and must not contain elements that could jeopardise public safety, harmony and well-being.
It urged all parties to allow the ministry space to manage publication-related matters in a balanced manner, taking into account both creative freedom and public interest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zeekr expands flagship SUV line-up with 8X
Article:
ZEEKR, a luxury technology brand under Geely Auto Group, has unveiled a new high-performance flagship SUV aimed at expanding its premium vehicle portfolio.
The 8X model joins the Zeekr 9X to form the brand's dual-flagship SUV lineup.
Built on the SEA-S architecture and powered by the Super Electric Power hybrid system, the 8X is positioned to combine strong performance, versatile handling and an upscale driving experience in the high-end SUV segment.
"Thanks to core technologies such as its full-stack 900V high-voltage architecture, high-performance AWD system, and Haohan AI digital chassis, the 8X delivers unmatched power across all speed ranges and steady control on all terrains," the company said.
The three-motor 8X Flagship Edition accelerates from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.96 seconds, making it the world's fastest-accelerating hybrid SUV.
"As a flagship five-seater, the Zeekr 8X shatters the conventional notion that 'performance and comfort are mutually exclusive' with MPV-like spaciousness, one-touch comfort of the Eames lounge chair and the world's only Naim audio system, offering a new luxury choice that balances driving performance with comfort," Zeekr said.
As the first model to feature a cabin-and-driving-integrated super intelligence system, the 8X is setting a new intelligence benchmark for the new energy era.
The voice assistant has evolved to Super Eva based on WAM (World Action Model), becoming a vehicle-wide super-intelligent control centre natively integrated with underlying systems such as G-ASD 4.0 driver-assistance system, chassis and powertrain.
The variants of Zeekr 8X are listed between 356,800 renmimbi to 426,800 renminbi, and the Flagship Edition is priced at 500,800 renminbi.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KTMB rolls out special Aidiladha train to East Coast
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) will operate a special 'Ekspres Lambaian Aidiladha' service to the East Coast to meet balik kampung demand ahead of Hari Raya Aidiladha next month.
In a statement today, KTMB said the service will comprise two trips, with tickets going on sale from 10am on April 24.
A total of 456 tickets will be available per trip, bringing the overall allocation to 912 tickets.
One-way fares range from RM80 to RM95, with options including premier and superior class seats, superior sleeper coaches, as well as chillax and café coaches.
The train will depart KL Sentral at 10pm on May 24 and arrive in Tumpat at 4.54pm the following day.
The return service will depart Tumpat at 8.30pm on May 29 and arrive at KL Sentral at 2.55pm the next day.
The train will stop at 16 stations, including Bandar Tasik Selatan, Kajang, Seremban, Gemas, Mentakab, Jerantut, Kuala Lipis, Gua Musang, Kuala Krai and Pasir Mas.
"KTMB will continue to support the government's initiative to expand rail transport options by providing special train services to help reduce congestion along the East Coast route, which is typically heavy during festive seasons, while making it easier for the public to return home comfortably and safely.
"The special service also provides opportunities for tourists planning to visit destinations in the East Coast," it said.
KTMB advised passengers to arrive early to allow time for security checks and entry procedures.
Tickets are available via the KTM Mobile (KITS) application, KTMB's official website and ticketing kiosks at selected stations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah raids expose illegal shops run by foreigners
Article:
ALOR STAR: Illegal immigrants of various nationalities have continued operating businesses in Kubang Pasu and Kuala Muda despite ongoing enforcement action.
They were found running premises such as car wash outlets, barber shops, bicycle stores and budget hotels.
Their activities were uncovered by the Kedah Immigration Department, which detained 10 foreigners during a series of operations — Ops Gegar, Ops Mahir, Ops Dandan, Ops Minyak and Ops Sapu — conducted in the two districts on Wednesday.
In a statement, it said the operations were carried out by its Immigration Action Unit under the Enforcement Division, involving 16 officers.
[[nid:1424970]]
A total of six premises were inspected, including car wash outlets, barber shops, bicycle stores and budget hotels.
"Inspections involved 30 individuals of various nationalities, resulting in the arrest of 10 foreigners comprising seven men and three women aged between 18 and 49.
"Those detained were from Myanmar, India, Bangladesh, Thailand and Indonesia.
"The offences identified include Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for not possessing valid travel documents, as well as Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963 for breaching pass conditions," the statement said.
All detainees were sent to the Belantik Immigration Depot for further action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sungai Muda hits danger level, Penang activates contingency plan
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang has averted a potential water supply disruption after the Sungai Muda river level plunged to a "danger level" of 1.27m at the Lahar Tiang intake yesterday.
Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the sharp drop was recorded about 4.30pm, well below the safe threshold of 2.0m.
He said the situation forced PBAPP to immediately scale down raw water abstraction from the usual 1,300 million litres per day (MLD) to about 1,000 MLD — a reduction of roughly 30 per cent.
"In response, we activated the Sungai Muda Contingency Plan and released about 300 MLD from the Expanded Mengkuang Dam (EMD) to sustain operations at the Sungai Dua Water Treatment Plant (WTP)," he said today.
The Sungai Dua plant, Penang's largest and most critical facility, produces an average of 1,100 MLD of treated water and supplies about 465,000 consumers across all five districts in the state.
Pathmanathan said the swift activation of the contingency plan had so far prevented supply disruptions in Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah, Seberang Perai Selatan, as well as the northeast and southwest districts on the island.
He attributed the sudden drop in river levels to upstream irrigation withdrawals in Kedah, adding that PBAPP had not been informed in advance.
As of yesterday, the Beris Dam — which feeds the Sungai Muda system — stood at 49.6 per cent capacity, with a release rate of 864 MLD. At that level, reserves are projected to last about 70 days before reaching the critical 20 per cent threshold.
Meanwhile, the Muda Dam's storage has fallen to 9.5 per cent and is not expected to contribute further releases in the immediate term.
Pathmanathan urged the National Water Services Commission and the Energy and Natural Resources Ministry to urgently intervene in managing the Sungai Muda system.
"The security of water supply for Penang and Kedah must take precedence over irrigation needs under current conditions," he said.
He said that without significant rainfall to replenish dam levels, the situation could deteriorate further, as Sungai Muda remains Penang's sole major raw water source.
"Any sustained inability to abstract sufficient water from the river could trigger a statewide disruption affecting 465,000 consumers, including industrial users and the tourism sector," he said.
While PBAPP will continue maximising abstraction from Sungai Muda and supplementing supply from the EMD, Pathmanathan cautioned that the dam's maximum drawdown capacity was limited to 600 MLD.
"The Sungai Dua plant requires about 1,300 MLD of raw water daily. Our mitigation measures are therefore finite," he said.
He reiterated his call for immediate action to curb upstream irrigation withdrawals until rainfall restores reservoir levels, stressing that safeguarding continuous water supply must remain the top priority.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foton to expand Malaysia push with V9 and V7 trucks
Article:
CHINA's Foton is set to expand its presence in Malaysia with the introduction of two new pickup truck models, marking the brand's latest push into the increasingly competitive lifestyle and utility vehicle segment.
The new Tunland V9 and Tunland V7 models are powered by a 2.0-litre turbo diesel mild-hybrid (MHEV) powertrain producing 161PS and 450Nm of torque, combining stronger acceleration with lower emissions.
The launch scheduled next month reflects a broader industry shift towards electrified technologies even within the pickup truck category, where fuel efficiency and environmental standards are becoming more important.
Positioned as large-format pickups, the Tunland V9 and V7 are among the biggest models in their class, with dimensions said to be comparable to larger American trucks such as the Ford F-150 and Ram 1500.
The higher-spec V9 variant comes with features uncommon in the segment, including a panoramic sunroof, rear multi-link coil suspension, ventilated leather seats with memory settings, and a 14.6-inch infotainment display.
Both models are equipped with a 12.3-inch digital instrument cluster, advanced driver assistance systems, a 360-degree camera and an eight-speed ZF Friedrichshafen automatic transmission with paddle shifters and six driving modes.
Practicality remains a key focus. The trucks offer a towing capacity of 3.5 tonnes, payload of close to one tonne and a large cargo bed fitted with a protective liner.
Fuel consumption is estimated at between eight and 10 litres per 100km, with a potential driving range of up to 950km from a 76-litre tank.
Maximum estimated price for Tunland V9 is RM148,888 before official price launch, while Tunland V7 is estimated at a maximum RM128,888 (before official price launch).
The company is also offering a seven-year or 200,000km warranty, signalling confidence in long-term ownership support.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India criticises 'poor taste' Trump post against immigrants
Article:
WASHINGTON: India on Thursday criticised as inappropriate a post by US President Donald Trump that called the South Asian country a "hellhole."
The comments come ahead of a planned visit next month to India by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who would seek to turn the page on recent tensions between the normally friendly powers.
Trump late Wednesday posted on social media a screed apparently written by someone else denouncing the US constitutional right to citizenship of everyone born in the country.
The post accused Indian immigrants in the tech industry of not hiring white native-born Americans and inaccurately alleged that Indian immigrants lack English proficiency.
"A baby here becomes an instant citizen, and then they bring the entire family in from China or India or some other hellhole on the planet," the post said.
Indian foreign ministry spokesman Randhir Jaiswal responded that the remarks were "obviously uninformed, inappropriate and in poor taste."
"They certainly do not reflect the reality of the India-US relationship, which has long been based on mutual respect and shared interests."
Congressman Ami Bera, a Democrat who is the children of Indian immigrants, called the post by Trump "offensive, ignorant and beneath the dignity of the office he holds."
"President Trump, who was born into wealth and privilege, has never had to struggle the way so many immigrant families have," he said.
The Hindu American Foundation, an advocacy group, said it was disturbed by the "hateful, racist screed."
"Endorsing such rants as the president of the United States will further stoke hatred and endanger our communities, at a time when xenophobia and racism are already at an all-time high," it wrote on X.
Trump, who has made a sweeping crackdown on immigration a signature policy, has taken aim at visas commonly used by Indian tech workers.
He also for months maintained major tariffs on India after he was angry that Prime Minister Narendra Modi downplayed his mediation during a conflict between India and Pakistan, which has avidly courted Trump.
Trump's sparring with India stands in contrast to decades of efforts by successive US presidents to avoid friction and build relations with the world's largest democracy, which US policymakers have seen as a counterweight to rival China. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Architecture graduate turns TV repairman to support family of six
Article:
KAJANG: It never crossed Khairul Anuar Ya'akub's mind that he would one day earn a living repairing televisions.
But a chance meeting with a friend changed his path, leading the 45-year-old to learn a skill that has since become his steady source of income.
Today, Khairul Anuar is able to support his wife, Nur Suhana Roslan, 40, and their four children aged between 8 and 16.
A graduate with a Bachelor's degree in Architecture from Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM), he said he had never been inclined towards salaried employment.
Over the years, he tried his hand at various ventures — from property and insurance sales to health products and children's clothing — before running an online business selling ''sambal telur sotong''.
The business initially took off, but supply disruptions during the Movement Control Order (MCO) pushed him to seek another source of income.
"I never planned to do this. I even wondered if anyone would want television repair services," he said.
"Then I met a friend, Zulkifli Majid, 49. I just wanted to chat, not talk about my problems.
"But he seemed to sense I was struggling and asked if I wanted to learn television repair. I laughed at first, but the very next day I went to learn from him."
Khairul Anuar said Zulkifli patiently taught him the trade, sharing his knowledge without hesitation despite running his own repair service nearby.
"I consider him my closest friend because he helped me at a difficult time," he said.
It took him six months to master the skill before he began working independently in November 2023.
"At first, I was nervous, but I pushed myself to take it seriously. I was made to open television sets every day until I got used to it," he said.
"Each unit is different, so you need patience and care. Without proper knowledge, you can manage it."
Khairul Anuar said his first workspace was his own bedroom, using only a screwdriver, before gradually saving up to buy proper tools.
He later moved his operations to the dining table and living room before eventually renting a premises in November last year, after two years of working from home.
"I still remember fixing televisions in my bedroom because I didn't want to be disturbed.
"When I managed to repair one, I would turn up the volume loudly. My children would hear it and run into the room," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fomca: Regulate e-commerce returns to curb abuse, protect sellers
Article:
PETALING JAYA: The government should regulate e-commerce platforms offering return and refund systems to prevent abuse that leaves sellers bearing losses, the Federation of Malaysian Consumers Associations (Fomca) said.
Its operations director, Nur Asyikin Aminuddin, said the issue is particularly worrying as sellers face improper returns, including empty boxes or items that do not match original orders.
She said this not only results in financial and inventory losses, but also erodes sellers' confidence in the online trading system.
Data from the National Consumer Complaints Centre (NCCC) showed complaints related to product returns were the highest in the e-commerce sector, with 704 cases involving losses of nearly RM300,000 up to 2024.
"Many cases occur when returned items are not the same as those delivered to customers. This has a significant impact on small traders," she told Utusan Malaysia yesterday.
The newspaper had previously reported that certain groups were exploiting "return and refund" terms offered by e-commerce platforms to obtain goods without paying.
While such policies are intended to protect consumers from fraud – such as items not matching descriptions – they have become a burden for sellers and small traders when misused.
The issue arises when customers return items that do not match the original order, including empty boxes or unrelated goods.
Nur Asyikin said consumers are entitled to refunds within a reasonable period, provided returns are made legitimately.
She added that all parties — including platforms, sellers and logistics providers — must ensure clear, accessible and transparent return and refund policies.
"Refunds should be processed within a reasonable timeframe once the returned item is received.
"Courier services must also ensure proper tracking and handling of returned goods to avoid disputes. Consumers should be promptly informed about the status of their returns and refunds," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kota Kinabalu man gets 15 months in jail for car theft disguised as purchase
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, April 24 — A 25-year-old man was sentenced to 15 months’ jail and fined RM2,000 or three months’ jail by the Magistrate’s Court here for stealing a car under the pretext of buying it.
Aedan Dylan John pleaded guilty before Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus to stealing a Perodua Kenari EZ (Auto) from one Soong Che Wai, at about 1.30pm on March 30, this year, at a taxi parking area of the Kota Kinabalu International Airport.
The charge under Section 379A of the Penal Code carries a jail term of between one and seven years and a fine, on conviction.
The court heard from the prosecution that Aedan had contacted the owner via WhatsApp after seeing an advertisement for a Perodua Kenari listed for sale on Facebook Marketplace for RM9,500.
He later met the owner at a house in Putatan to inspect the vehicle and agreed to purchase it.
Aedan then persuaded the owner to go to the airport, claiming he needed to withdraw cash from an ATM to make payment.
He asked to test-drive the car and requested that they travel separately.
However, when arriving at the airport, Aedan drove off with the vehicle and fled the scene.
The owner later discovered that his phone number had been blocked by Aedan.
Police arrested Aedan on April 3, at 4.30pm in Sipitang.
The stolen vehicle and its key were recovered following the arrest.
An identification parade was later conducted, during which the complainant positively identified Aedan.
The vehicle was also identified based on matching chassis details.
The court ordered Aedan to serve the sentence from the date of his arrest.
In another case, Aedan claimed trial to a charge of dishonestly misappropriating a car belonging to a company.
Aedan was accused of selling a Perodua Myvi owned by Seri Pancar Engineering Sdn Bhd to another individual at the UTC building, Jalan Belia, Off Jalan Tunku Abdul Rahman, here, on March 12, this year.
The charge under Section 403 of the Penal Code carries a jail term of between six months and five years, with whipping, and may also include a fine, on conviction.
The prosecution in applying for a pre-trial case manangement (PTCM) proposed bail at RM10,000.
The court fixed June 22 for PTCM pre-trial and granted bail of RM6,000 in two sureties.
Inspector Alia Nuurlysa Adnan prosecuted. — Daily Express

Title: Police detain foreigner in Niah after threat to kill sister, seize machete and homemade shotgun
Article:
MIRI, April 24 — Police have arrested a 37-year-old foreign man for allegedly threatening to kill his younger sister with a machete in their home.
Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) received a report at 1.44pm on April 22 from the 35-year-old sister.
He said the suspect allegedly threatened her during an incident at their residence in an Ulu Suai, Niah plantation.
“Acting on the information received, a team of officers and personnel from CID Miri conducted a raid at a farm near Wilmar Suai 1 oil palm plantation in Suai and successfully arrested the suspect.
“Police also seized a machete, a homemade shotgun, and six rounds of ammunition during the operation,” he said in a statement.
Mohd Farhan said the suspect has been remanded for four days until April 27 to assist in investigations.
A urine test on the suspect was positive for drugs.
The case is being investigated under Section 506 of the Penal Code for criminal intimidation, which carries a jail term of up to seven years, or a fine, or both upon conviction, as well as Section 8(a) of the Arms Act 1960, which provides for imprisonment of up to seven years, or a fine not exceeding RM10,000, or both.
Mohd Farhan stressed that police take all public reports seriously and urged those with information regarding the homemade shotgun to contact investigating officer Insp Mohd Rayhan Albert on 011-10846969 or 085-435081, or to go to the nearest police station. — The Borneo Post

Title: Selangor records 209 water theft cases last year, most involving commercial accounts
Article:
SHAH ALAM, April 24 — A total of 209 water theft cases were reported in Selangor last year, with most involving commercial accounts, the State Legislative Assembly was told today.
State Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said 184 of the cases involved commercial accounts, while the remaining 25 involved domestic accounts.
He said 43 water theft cases had been recorded as of March this year, involving 12 domestic accounts and 31 commercial accounts.
“Water theft not only affects the supply system and the interests of legitimate users, but also has a direct impact on non-revenue water rates, water pressure stability and the integrity of the existing pipe network.
“As such, the state government, through the Air Selangor Enforcement Section, has carried out continuous monitoring, intelligence actions, and enforcement in accordance with the laws stipulated under the Water Services Industry Act 2006 (Act 655) to curb water theft through several strategies,” he said at the state assembly sitting.
Izham was replying to an oral question from Mohd Razali Saari (PN-Sungai Panjang), who asked about developments in water theft cases and measures taken to address the issue.
He said measures implemented by the state government included daily preventive action through proactive monitoring in high-risk areas, responding to complaints and reports from frontliners, community collaboration, official complaints to Pengurusan Air Selangor Sdn Bhd via smartphone applications, and wider cooperation efforts.
He added that integrated operations were also carried out by Air Selangor with the National Water Services Commission, local authorities and the media. — Bernama

Title: Police arrest three teenagers in Tawau over alleged rape of 15-year-old schoolgirl
Article:
TAWAU, April 24 — Police have arrested three teenage boys, aged between 15 and 16, to assist in investigations into the alleged rape of a 15-year-old schoolgirl here.
District Police Chief ACP Jasmin Hussin said the suspects, including the main suspect, were detained at about 6.50pm on Wednesday at the Merotai police outpost.
He said the arrests were made within 24 hours after a police report was lodged by the victim’s mother at about 4.43pm the same day.
“Police moved swiftly to locate the suspects following the report,” he said yesterday.
Initial investigations indicate that the victim was allegedly taken by one of the suspects, a Form Five student, in a car before the incident occurred at a house.
The case came to light after the victim, a Form Three student, confided in her school counsellor, who then informed her mother.
Police also detained two other suspects believed to have been involved in the incident.
All three suspects are being held at the Tawau district police headquarters, with the case being investigated under Section 376 of the Penal Code.
Jasmin said police view sexual offences seriously and urged parents to monitor their children’s movements and social circles to prevent such incidents. — Daily Express
* If you are experiencing sexual violence, the following hotlines offer free and confidential support: Talian Kasih at 15999 or WhatsApp 019-2615999 (24/7); All Women’s Action Society at 016-2374221/016-2284221 (9.30am-5.30pm); and Women’s Aid Organisation (WAO) at 03-30008858 or SMS/WhatsApp TINA 018-9888058 (24/7)

Title: Penang CM urges cloud seeding as critically low Sungai Muda levels threaten state water supply
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, April 24 — The Penang state government hopes cloud seeding operations can be carried out in upstream Kedah to raise water levels in Sungai Muda.
Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the measure was important to ensure sufficient raw water supply in the state.
“The river’s water level has dropped to a critical stage,” he said at a press conference after launching the Penang ATE campus at Olive Tree Hotel here today.
Chow said the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) had contacted authorities in Kedah to coordinate immediate action.
Apart from cloud seeding, he said releasing water from Beris Dam in Kedah had also been proposed as a short-term measure.
He added that Penang would channel water from the Expanded Mengkuang Dam to treatment plants to ensure consumer supply remains unaffected.
Earlier today, PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said Sungai Muda’s level at Lahar Tiang fell to a danger level of 1.27 metres yesterday, compared with the safe level of two metres.
He said under normal conditions, PBAPP abstracts about 1,300 million litres per day (MLD) of raw water from Sungai Muda for use at the Sungai Dua Water Treatment Plant.
“From 4.30pm yesterday, PBAPP’s abstraction capacity was limited to 1,000 MLD, or 30 per cent below the normal level due to the abnormally low river level,” he said.
He said PBAPP immediately began drawing water from the Expanded Mengkuang Dam to supplement supply to the Sungai Dua plant.
He said the drop in river level could be due to irrigation drawdowns in Kedah upstream of the Lahar Tiang intake.
“As of April 23, the capacity of the Beris Dam (which is releasing water into the Sungai Muda system) was 49.6 per cent,” he said.
He said the release rate was reportedly 864 MLD, meaning reserves may last 70 days before reaching the critical 20 per cent level.
He added that reserves at Muda Dam, another key source for the Sungai Muda system, had fallen to 9.5 per cent.
He called on the National Water Services Commission (SPAN) and the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (PETRA) to intervene in managing the Sungai Muda system.
“The security of water supply services in Penang and Kedah must be prioritised over irrigation matters under the present circumstances,” Pathmanathan said.

Title: High Court sets May 14 for decision on Zahid’s bid for acquittal in Akalbudi case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — The decision on Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi’s application to be discharged and acquitted of 47 charges of criminal breach of trust, corruption and money laundering involving Yayasan Akalbudi funds will be delivered on May 14.
High Court Judge Nurulhuda Nur’aini Mohamad Nor fixed the date after counsel Datuk Hamidi Mohd Noh, representing Ahmad Zahid, informed the court that the defence had applied to vacate today’s decision date pending the Court of Appeal’s ruling on May 7.
“The court sets May 14 for the decision, subject to the outcome of the Court of Appeal on May 7,” she said.
Earlier, Hamidi said that on April 20, the defence had filed a letter seeking to vacate today’s decision date pending the appellate court’s ruling.
He added that Ahmad Zahid was exempted from attending today’s proceedings. — Bernama

Title: Bukit Aman CID: Bangsar’s Jalan Telawi shooting tied to revenge over past gang turf disputes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — A shooting along Jalan Telawi in Bangsar on April 22 is believed to have stemmed from revenge linked to past territorial disputes between organised crime groups, police have confirmed.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said a 41-year-old local man was shot in the abdomen following an altercation between two groups outside an entertainment outlet during the incident.
The victim’s condition is stable, Kumar said, adding that the two suspects arrested over the incident are currently in remand.
“Acting on intelligence, police conducted a raid at a hotel in Klang, Selangor and seized two firearms inside a hotel room.
“Both suspects aged 27 and 33 were arrested in the morning of April 23, less than 24 hours after the shooting,” he told a press conference at the federal police headquarters in Bukit Aman here.
Previously, Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said a pistol with one live round and a replica firearm were seized during the raid.
Kumar said police did not dismiss the possibility of the suspects’ involvement in organised crime activities, but believed the case to be solved with the suspects’ arrest.

Title: Selangor to adopt Year One entry at age six from 2027, hire teachers for influx
Article:
SHAH ALAM, April 24 — The Selangor state government will adopt the Education Ministry's Year One pupil admission policy starting at age six for the 2027 school session, including primary schools handled by the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (JAIS).
Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari informed the state assembly today that plans are in place to hire more teachers and upgrade school facilities to meet the influx of Year One students.
He said the preparations include proposals to track and optimise classroom use, allowing up to 45 pupils per class.
“Secondly, coordinating student data to pinpoint densely populated areas alongside the Selangor State Education Department. And thirdly, identifying the need for more teachers,” he said during today's Selangor State Assembly sitting.
Amirudin responded to an oral question from Noor Najhan Mohamad Salleh (PN-Sementa) on JAIS teacher and facility preparations for the 2027 six-year-old Year One intake under Education Ministry guidelines.
Meanwhile, Amirudin said other proposals include switching to three school sessions from two, where suitable, and adding infrastructure such as extra classrooms to cope with the rise in student numbers.
“We expect nearly 80,000 Year One applications for the 2027 school session and a shortfall of 880 teachers.
“Now, one of our main strategies is hiring more teachers to fill the gap, depending on the state's capacity and position,” he said.
Meanwhile, he said all JAIS school buildings are safe and suitable, with the state government ready to fund repairs if schools or the JAIS Education Division request it.
On January 20, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced at the National Education Blueprint (RPN) 2026-2035 launch that starting next year, six-year-olds can enter Year One, optional and up to parents. — Bernama

Title: Penang LRT Mutiara Line construction on track, says CM amid political interference claims
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, April 24 — The Penang LRT Mutiara Line project remains on schedule despite the tender process for Package Two still pending finalisation.
Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said works on Package One are ongoing, dismissing claims that political interference had caused delays.
“The civil and structural work is still underway, the construction of the pier is still underway, not completed yet so the next package can only start after this first package is completed, we cannot build the track when the pier is not completed,” he told reporters after launching the Penang ATE Campus at Olive Tree Hotel here today.
Asked about reports of political interference in the tender process for Package Two, Chow Kon Yeow said the state government was not involved, as the process is handled by project owner MRT Corp.
There were allegations that Package Two had been delayed due to lobbying by different political groups with the Transport Ministry and Finance Ministry.
He said the state government is only a stakeholder in the project through the Procurement One Stop Centre Committee, while MRT Corp will evaluate all bids before submitting a proposal for approval.
“After MRT Corp makes the decision, they will submit their recommendations to the One Stop Procurement Centre but I can say, it is not up to this level yet,” he said.
The LRT Mutiara Line is planned to span almost 30km from Silicon Island in southern Penang island to George Town, and also connect to Penang Sentral in Butterworth on the mainland.

Title: Penang cements role as Malaysia’s automation, test and equipment hub with launch of collaborative campus initiative
Article:
